item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On your cons, as i read them they are all but one explained in more depth on the footnotes, at least all those problems you posted i found exact answers reading again the relevant part and it's footnotes.

I agree on your point that Foundation shouldn't trust them, but it's written in a way that makes you think they almost dismissed it.

I still realy like it.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: As the first image has been deleted, there is no indication it is still available under Creative Commons, and the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pros:
-An interesting story. Small town politics with geopolitical parallels, and XK-class possible repercussions. It's solid social commentary if nothing else.
-The Foundation approaches the situation thoughtfully. So many portal-to-another-world skips have staff just blindly exploring until they get eaten by boogeymen, or shooting anything that comes through with no questions asked. Here they investigated cautiously, made contact with the natives, and tried to work with local authorities to proactively prevent passage. Didn't work, but it was still the right thing to try.

Cons:
-The explanation is muddled. I re-read the relevant section a few times and I'm still not clear if any visitor causes an explosion when they arrive, or if reality collapse depends on if they are in group A, B, or C. I believe it's politics-independent, but there shouldn't be any doubt about something so basic to the concept.
-The mayor's brother causes reality distortion in a 2 meter radius, which returns to normal when he leaves the radius. But how can he leave an area effect that is centered on him? I think it was trying to say that the effect moves with B5, that everything goes back to normal behind him as he walks around. But as written it just makes no sense.
-When The Foundation got new intel from B5, they should have adjusted their operations. Security would need to know not to fully trust Bs at the very least. Unless they had strong evidence that he was lying, in which case that should be a note after the interview log.
-In the last interview, why would the mayor's brother not be able to go back? B1 doesn't know he died from reality failure, and there are no side-effects when anyone goes thru the portal into the pocket dimension. So why wouldn't the mayor's "friends" at The Foundation just take bro home if they found him?

Net score: -1, but it's a close thing and I'm clearly in the minority.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should put more details in the footnote citations. Referring to an article just by journal name and year is unreasonable…add the issue number and page number.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good idea, poor execution. Wording and tone mostly although in general I think this would be better as a Tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was really expecting the last collapsible to go someplace with that, and instead it almost made it seem like the mayor feels guilty about everything, but won't say what he did.

Upvoting, because it's awesome either way, but I do wish there was more done with the reversal there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's like dropping a couple of bricks when you're trying to make a small building. It's there but with nothing leading up to it or anything following it it's just a bit ehh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1095-2-B-5: The people you are hunting after … they are the wrong ones.

He states here that the radicals are not the ones they should be witch hunting. This is coming from a close relative of the mayor who's running the town.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thing is, there's very little to no indication of this in the skip.

Spoilers I guess?
– hide block

The most you have is that 2B is going on a witch hunt to find these radicals, but the radicals themselves are perfectly legitimate. If the radicals are fake, the Foundation should know about this to some extent. But it's hard to pick up on because, well, there's not enough clues to lead to this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoilerz
- Close

The radicals are phony. The 2B instances are the one who've wanted to pass through the portal all along. They're the ones who opened up the portal in the first place.

The mayor was running the town with this in mind, until his brother vanished (and later died. But he doesn't know that). It can be assumed that either the mayor ran off to find his brother, or execute the hidden plan of "let me cross into this world so I can use my powers freely".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like there's something more going on here than "town of reality benders", but I can't figure out what. I do like the extensive political system you've set up, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, although I wish more had been done with it, somehow. Upvoted, anyway. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Didn't read this before, but I did see the 'town of reality benders' idea in the forums and wondered how it would be written without descending into cliches. This is definitely good work.

I will say that the dialogue is a tad choppy, and going over it one more time wouldn't hurt, but it's not bad enough to prevent an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've discussed how I view the Foundation containing this thing versus how I think it should be contained with you. I don't see why the Foundation continues to interfere with it; once they get all the knowledge they need, they could simply seal the doors shut and shoot every living thing that pokes its head out and tries to reality-bend out to death. Of course, the way you approach it is your prerogative, so I can't really fault you for that. The rest of the article is alright to carry even the downsides, so I just have to novote. Good job though.

item.title: Re: Author Note
item.content:encoded:
Charles Royer! I've read this in chat.

Upvoted.

item.title: Author Note
item.content:encoded:
Hey guys! Here's a new skip. Thanks to TwistedGears, Cris_To_Baal, DrLeonard, Djoric, Decibelles, and everyone else who looked over my draft.

Thanks to Creative Commons, I used these images:

cc (Tavern): https://www.flickr.com/photos/collum/315337218/
cc (Mayor): https://www.flickr.com/photos/seattlemunicipalarchives/3274612121
cc (Town Fire): http://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_town_hall_fire_in_Kwidzyn,_Poland.jpg
